commit.message:
feat: Phase 20 — NLP Command Parser

- parseVoiceCommand(): 13 intent categories, keyword weight scoring
- levenshtein() helper for fuzzy spell/resource name matching
- Entity extraction: dice notation, spell name, skill, save, condition, amount, advantage/disadvantage
- Alternates: top-2 suggestions when scores within 0.15
- 30+ table-driven unit tests
- socketServer: final transcripts → command:parsed event
- CommandFeedback: Did you mean? UI with auto-dispatch + 4s timeout
- useCommandParser: high-confidence auto-dispatch, medium → confirm
- TranscriptPanel: intent badges on final transcripts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set alertmanager.data_dir and ruler.rule_path to /tmp to avoid subdirectory overlaps

Both alertmanager.data_dir and ruler.rule_path default to /data.
Their storage dirs (/data/alertmanager, /data/ruler) are subdirectories
of /data — Mimir rejects this. Working dirs are ephemeral scratch space
so /tmp is correct; persisted config stays on the PVC under /data/*.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add Web Speech API global type declarations for TypeScript

window.SpeechRecognition and webkitSpeechRecognition are not declared
in TypeScript's lib.dom.d.ts by default, causing TS2339 in CI.
Added a global augmentation declaration file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 19 — Voice Recognition (STT)

- Add shared STT types: VoiceTranscriptEvent, VoiceMode
- Add ISTTProvider port with transcribeChunk, startStream, sendAudioToStream, endStream
- Add MockSTTProvider adapter for testing
- Add DeepgramSTTProvider adapter using @deepgram/sdk v5
- Add STTService wiring socket IDs to provider streams
- Add STTService unit tests (9 passing)
- Register sttProvider and sttService in Awilix container

- Add voice socket events: voice:start, voice:audio_chunk, voice:stop
- Track currentSessionId per socket for voice event context
- Auto-cleanup streams on socket disconnect
- Add useSpeechRecognition hook (Web Speech API + MediaRecorder fallback)

- Add VoiceButton unit tests (7 passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: separate alertmanager_storage and ruler_storage paths in Mimir

common.storage.filesystem.dir=/data sets the default for all components.
alertmanager and ruler both default their storage to /data, causing path
overlap validation errors. Give each its own subdirectory.

  /data/alertmanager  — alertmanager state
  /data/ruler         — ruler recordings and alerts
  /data/blocks        — metrics block storage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: serverden-homelab

Key focuses: - GitOps workflows and automation - Kubernetes manifests and deployment samples - Infrastructure-as-Code experiments - Prototyping new tooling and approaches to cloud-native infrastructure

commit.message:
fix: disable mimir rollout-operator in monolithic mode

The rollout-operator registers a mutating webhook with a broken self-signed
cert (no SANs), blocking all applies. It is only needed for distributed
stateful component coordination — unnecessary in monolithic (target: all) mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: serverden-homelab
repository.description: This repository contains GitOps, Kubernetes, and various prototyping resources for infrastructure training and experimentation.  Key focuses: - GitOps workflows and automation - Kubernetes manifests and deployment samples - Infrastructure-as-Code experiments - Prototyping new tooling and approaches to cloud-native infrastructure

commit.message:
fix: replace multi-source ref with single-source valuesObject for all monitoring apps

Multi-source ref requires the git repo to be explicitly registered in
ArgoCD as a connected repository even for public repos, causing
'failed to get git client' errors. Switch to single Helm source with
helm.valuesObject to inline values — simpler and always reliable.

Affects: mimir, loki, tempo, grafana, alloy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: disable Loki chunksCache and resultsCache for SingleBinary homelab

Memcached cache components are enabled by default but unnecessary in
SingleBinary mode. They were causing pods to get stuck in Pending state.

commit.message:
fix: replace invalid stage.kubernetes with discovery.relabel in Alloy config

stage.kubernetes does not exist in Alloy River DSL (Promtail concept).
Use discovery.relabel before loki.source.kubernetes to promote
__meta_kubernetes_* labels to plain Loki stream labels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: switch API tsconfig to ESNext+bundler for shared extensionless imports

The API tsconfig used NodeNext moduleResolution which requires .js
extensions in all relative imports. Since packages/shared was switched to
bundler resolution (extensionless imports for Turbopack compat), the API
typecheck failed when processing shared source files via the path alias.

Changing the API to module:ESNext+moduleResolution:bundler resolves this:
- Bundler resolution accepts both extensionless and .js imports
- Existing API source .js extensions are still valid
- Shared source extensionless imports now compile without TS2835 errors
- Next.js Turbopack remains happy with extensionless shared imports

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Standardize server-side API endpoints to v1 pattern

Change 1: Add v1 prefix to 5 non-versioned controllers
- BrandsController, LotsController, ModelsController,
  StorageLocationsController: [Route("api/[controller]")] -> [Route("api/v1/[controller]")]
- ClientLogsController: same route fix + add CancellationToken to async actions

Change 2: ExportController - inherit BaseApiController
- Change ControllerBase -> BaseApiController
- Replace StatusCode(500, "Export failed") with CreateInternalServerErrorProblem(...)
- Replace BadRequest("Invalid options"/"No data") with CreateValidationProblemDetails(...)
- Add ProducesResponseType attributes (200, 400, 500)
- Add CancellationToken = default to action parameter

Change 3 & 5: DashboardConfigurationController
- Add CancellationToken cancellationToken = default to all async actions
- Pass cancellationToken to all service calls
- Replace StatusCode(500, ...) with CreateInternalServerErrorProblem(...)
- Replace NotFound() with CreateNotFoundProblem(...) for all 404 paths
- Add missing ProducesResponseType(Status500InternalServerError) on all actions

Change 4: SettingsController
- Add CancellationToken cancellationToken = default to all async actions
- Pass cancellationToken to all EF Core async calls and SqlConnection calls
- Add ProducesResponseType attributes to all action methods
- Replace StatusCode(500, ...) raw strings with CreateInternalServerErrorProblem(...)
  or Problem(...) where no exception is available

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ivanopaulon <115718393+ivanopaulon@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use moduleResolution bundler in shared pkg for Next.js Turbopack compat

Switch packages/shared to module: ESNext + moduleResolution: bundler and
remove .js extensions from all internal imports. This allows Next.js
Turbopack (via transpilePackages) to resolve shared source TypeScript files
without the .js → .ts remapping issue that was breaking CI web builds.

All 270 tests still pass (103 shared + 91 api + 76 web).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use transpilePackages + source exports for @local-dungeon/shared in Next.js

- packages/shared/package.json: change exports 'import' to 'default' pointing at
  src/index.ts — Turbopack can now use the TypeScript source directly
- apps/web/next.config.ts: add transpilePackages: ['@local-dungeon/shared']
  so Next.js compiles the workspace package from source instead of dist/
- CI: remove 'Build shared package' step from web job (no longer needed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix CancellationToken propagation in client-side service layer

- IStockReconciliationService/StockReconciliationService: add ct to
  CalculateReconciliationAsync, ApplyReconciliationAsync,
  ExportReconciliationAsync; fix RebuildMovements* to pass ct
- IEntityManagementService/EntityManagementService: add ct to all 23
  missing methods (addresses, contacts, references, classification nodes)
- IDocumentHeaderService/DocumentHeaderService: add ct to all 11 methods
- IModelService/ModelService: add ct to GetModels, GetModelsByBrandId,
  GetModelById, CreateModel, UpdateModel, DeleteModel; thread through cache
- ITransferOrderService/TransferOrderService: add ct to GetTransferOrder,
  CreateTransferOrder, ShipTransferOrder, ReceiveTransferOrder,
  CancelTransferOrder
- BrandManagementService: pass ct to GetBrandsAsync and DeleteBrandAsync
- TransferOrderManagementService: pass ct to CancelTransferOrderAsync

All new parameters use = default (non-breaking). Existing callers
compile unchanged. Build: 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ivanopaulon <115718393+ivanopaulon@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: build shared package before Next.js build in CI web job

Next.js Turbopack resolves @local-dungeon/shared from dist/index.js
(per package.json exports). Without a build step, dist/ doesn't exist
in CI and the build fails with 'Module not found'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add @local-dungeon/shared alias to web Vitest config

Without this alias, Vitest tries to resolve @local-dungeon/shared from
packages/shared/dist/ which doesn't exist in CI (not built).
Use path.resolve to point directly at the source, matching the API config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Docs: document auto-draw-on-play rule for non-combat disciplines

explaining the two distinct draw models:

- All other disciplines: auto-draw 1 card per play

- mining_balance.md: clarify 'both sides draw' wording
- designer.rs: split Draw effects into combat-only and auto-draw entries

Co-authored-by: gowdham-k <10788093+gowdham-k@users.noreply.github.com>

Add DocumentPreviewPage for public document viewing and signing

Public page at /documents/preview/:id/:token that allows recipients
to view composed documents and sign them without authentication.

Features:
- PDFVILLE branding header, centered layout (max-w-3xl)
- Read-only block rendering: paragraph, headings, table, image,
  signature, date, line_items, divider, page_break
- Sign section with name/email inputs (only for documents with signers)
- Handles loading, error, expired, and already-signed states

* fix: set Queued status before writing to channel in InMemoryBulkOperationQueue

status (Failed/Completed) before QueueAsync set initial Queued status,
overwriting the terminal status and causing flaky test failures.

commit.message:
Add `specify integration` subcommand for post-init integration management (#2083)

* Initial plan

* Add specify integration subcommand (list, install, uninstall, switch)

Implements the `specify integration` subcommand group for managing
integrations in existing projects after initial setup:

- `specify integration list` — shows available integrations and installed status
- `specify integration install <key>` — installs an integration into existing project
- `specify integration uninstall [key]` — hash-safe removal preserving modified files
- `specify integration switch <target>` — uninstalls current, installs target

Follows the established `specify <noun> <verb>` CLI pattern used by
extensions and presets. Shared infrastructure (scripts, templates) is
preserved during uninstall and switch operations.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/spec-kit/sessions/1cca6c84-3e12-465d-88b8-a646d3504f63

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: extract helper, fix return type annotation

- Extract _update_init_options_for_integration() to deduplicate init-options
  update logic between install and switch commands
- Fix _parse_integration_options return type to dict[str, Any] | None

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/spec-kit/sessions/1cca6c84-3e12-465d-88b8-a646d3504f63

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding 'Unused import'

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <223894421+github-code-quality[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: validate script type, handle --flag=value, fix metadata cleanup

- Add _normalize_script_type() to validate script type against SCRIPT_TYPE_CHOICES
- Handle --name=value syntax in _parse_integration_options()
- Clear init-options.json keys in no-manifest uninstall early-return path
- Clear stale metadata between switch teardown and install phases
- Add 5 tests covering the new edge cases

* Block --force with different integration, persist script type in init-options

- --force on install now rejects overwriting a different integration; users must
  use 'specify integration switch' instead
- _update_init_options_for_integration() now accepts and persists script_type
- Fix misleading test docstring for switch metadata test
- Add test_force_blocked_with_different_integration

* Remove --force from integration install, ensure shared infra on install/switch

- Remove --force parameter entirely from integration install; users must
  uninstall before reinstalling to prevent orphaned files
- Auto-install shared infrastructure (.specify/scripts/, .specify/templates/)
  when missing during install or switch
- Add test for shared infra creation on bare project install

* Remove redundant installed_key != key check

The == key case already returns above, so the != key guard is always true
at this point. Simplify to just 'if installed_key:'.

* Run shared infra unconditionally, defer metadata removal in switch

- Call _install_shared_infra() unconditionally on install and switch since it
  merges without overwriting existing files
- Remove premature metadata cleanup between switch phases; metadata is now
  only updated after successful Phase 2 install

* Add install rollback, graceful manifest errors, clear switch metadata

- Attempt teardown rollback on install/switch failure to avoid orphaned files
- Catch ValueError/FileNotFoundError on IntegrationManifest.load() in uninstall
  with user-friendly recovery guidance
- Clear metadata immediately after switch teardown so failed Phase 2 doesn't
  leave stale references to the removed integration

* Log rollback failures instead of silently suppressing them

* Handle corrupt manifest in switch, distinguish unknown vs missing manifest

- Wrap IntegrationManifest.load() in switch with ValueError/FileNotFoundError
  handling, matching the pattern used in uninstall
- Split else branch to report 'unknown integration' vs 'no manifest' separately

* Clean up metadata on rollback, broaden init-options match in uninstall

- Remove integration.json in install/switch rollback paths so failed installs
  don't leave stale metadata
- Match on both 'integration' and 'ai' keys when clearing init-options.json
  during uninstall to handle partially-written metadata

* Fix recovery guidance for unreadable manifests, fix type annotations

- Recovery instructions now guide users through delete manifest → uninstall →
  reinstall workflow that actually works
- Type annotations for optional CLI parameters changed from str to str | None

* Allow manifest-only uninstall for unknown/removed integrations

- Uninstall no longer requires the integration to be in the registry; falls back
  to manifest.uninstall() directly when get_integration() returns None
- Switch Phase 1 similarly uses manifest-only uninstall for unknown integrations
  instead of skipping teardown, preventing orphaned files

* Fail fast on corrupt integration.json, validate integration options

- _read_integration_json() now exits with an actionable error when
  integration.json exists but is corrupt/unreadable
- _parse_integration_options() rejects unknown options, validates flag usage,
  and requires values for non-flag options

* Validate integration.json is a dict, fail fast on missing manifest in switch

- _read_integration_json() validates parsed JSON is a dict, not a list/string
- Switch fails fast with recovery guidance when manifest is missing instead
  of silently skipping teardown and risking co-existing integration files

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <223894421+github-code-quality[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use path.resolve for shared alias in API Vitest config

Hardcoded absolute path '/Users/guillaumecristiani/...' only worked locally.
Use path.resolve(__dirname, ...) so CI resolves correctly on any machine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove explicit rootDir from API tsconfig to allow cross-package type resolution

tsc --noEmit with paths alias resolves shared source files from outside
apps/api/src, causing TS6059 when rootDir is explicitly set to 'src'.
Removing rootDir lets TypeScript infer it from the union of included files,
which includes both apps/api/src and packages/shared/src via path resolution.
This works for --noEmit (CI typecheck) without needing to pre-build shared.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: CI — remove tsconfig project references, strip all .js extensions from Next.js imports

- apps/api/tsconfig.json: remove 'references' array (tsc --noEmit with project
  references requires pre-built dist/; paths alias alone is sufficient for typecheck)
- CI: remove 'Build shared package' step (no longer needed without project references)
- apps/web: strip .js extensions from all relative imports (./  and ../ prefixed)
  useSessionSocket.ts, session test files now all use extensionless imports

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: IssueTrackerApp
repository.description: An Aspire and Blazor solution for tracking users issues with the ability to comment and vote on the solution

commit.message:
feat: Phase 8 — session frontend & lobby

- Session API helpers: createSession, listSessions, getSession, joinSession, leaveSession
- TanStack Query hooks: useSessionList, useSessionInfo, useCreateSession, useJoinSession, useLeaveSession
- useSocket hook: manages socket.io-client connection with JWT auth, auto-reconnect
- useSessionSocket hook: joins/leaves rooms, receives players_updated + state_update events, ping util
- /sessions page: session grid, status badges, join modal with character select
- /sessions/new page: react-hook-form + zod, maxPlayers selector
- /sessions/:id page: game room with header bar, phase indicator, player sidebar, lobby/active states
- PlayerList component: player cards with online dot, ready toggle, disconnected overlay, empty slots
- MSW handlers for all 5 session endpoints
- Tests: PlayerList, SessionList, CreateSession, SessionRoom (4 test files, 53 total passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 7 — session infrastructure backend

- Session, SessionPlayer, GameEvent Prisma models
- ISessionRepository, IGameEventRepository port interfaces
- PrismaSessionRepository: full CRUD with player joins and character details
- PrismaGameEventRepository: create + findBySession
- RedisSessionStore: session state, player room membership, user→session mapping
- SessionService: create (invite code), join (by id or code), leave, getInfo, listByUser
- 5 JWT-protected REST endpoints: POST|GET /sessions, GET|POST|DELETE /sessions/:id
- Socket.io server with @socket.io/redis-adapter (horizontal scale ready)
- JWT auth middleware on WebSocket connection
- session:join, session:leave, game:ping/pong Socket.io events
- Unit tests: SessionService (8 cases), sessionHandler (5 routes), socketServer (ping/pong + auth)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 6 — character sheet UI

- InlineEdit component: click-to-edit with optimistic update, revert on error
- useCharacter / usePatchCharacter / useDeleteCharacter hooks (TanStack Query)
- Dashboard: character grid with HP bar, delete modal, Bring to Session placeholder
- Character Sheet (/characters/:id):
  - Identity block: name, class, level, bloodied indicator, heroic inspiration toggle
  - Core Stats bar: AC, initiative, speed, HP (editable), proficiency bonus, passive perception
  - Tab 1 Stats: ability scores (editable), saving throws (proficiency toggle), skills (18 rows)
  - Tab 2 Combat: attacks table, conditions badges, exhaustion
  - Tab 3 Spells: slot pips, cantrips, spells by level (spellcasters only)
  - Tab 4 Inventory: currency (editable), encumbrance bar, items table, equipment slots
  - Tab 5 Features: class features + feats
  - Tab 6 Profile: personality, appearance, backstory (all inline-editable)
- MSW handlers for GET/PATCH/DELETE /characters/:id
- Tests: InlineEdit, CharacterSheet, Dashboard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 5 — character creation wizard frontend

- WizardStore (Zustand + persist): 8-step state management
- useWizard hook: step navigation, per-step validation
- WizardProgress component: 8-step progress indicator
- Step 1: Class picker grid with expandable details
- Step 2: Background picker with equipment toggle
- Step 3: Species picker (2024: no ASI here)
- Step 4: Ability scores — Standard Array / Point Buy / Roll + background bonuses
- Step 5: Skills & feats (origin feat locked, class skill picker)
- Step 6: Spells picker (spellcasters only)
- Step 7: Finalize — name, alignment, appearance, personality, review panel
- Step 8: Submit to POST /characters, redirect on success
- Dashboard updated with character list and Create New button
- Tests: useWizard hook, Step1Class, Step4AbilityScores

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 4 — character creation backend

- Character Prisma model with all fields (stats, inventory, spells, features JSONB)
- packages/shared/rules/: pure derived stats engine
  - computeAbilityModifier, computeAllModifiers
  - computeProficiencyBonus (levels 1-20)
  - computeMaxHP, computeAC (5 modes), computeInitiative
  - computeSpellSaveDC, computeSpellAttackBonus
  - computeSkillModifier, computePassivePerception, computeCarryingCapacity
- ICharacterRepository port + PrismaCharacterRepository
- CharacterService: create/findAllByUser/findById/update/delete
- POST|GET|PATCH|DELETE /characters endpoints (JWT protected)
- Rules engine unit tests (edge cases: all AC modes, levels 1-20, CON 8-20)
- CharacterService unit tests with mocked repositories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 3 — reference data (D&D 2024 PHB content + API + browser)

- Prisma schema: DndClass, Subclass, Species, Background, Feat, Spell, Weapon, Armor, Condition, Language
- JSON seed data in packages/shared/data/ (PHB 2024 accurate):
  - 12 classes with subclasses and spell slot tables
  - 10 species, 16 backgrounds, ~70 feats, ~50 spells
  - 37 weapons (with 2024 mastery properties), 13 armor types
  - 15 conditions (2024 Exhaustion), 19 languages
- Idempotent seed script (prisma/seed.ts)
- IGameDataRepository port + PrismaGameDataRepository
- GameDataService with spell filtering
- GET /game-data/* endpoints (10 routes)
- Reference browser page (/reference) with tabs: Spells/Classes/Conditions/Weapons/Feats
- TanStack Query with staleTime: Infinity for reference data
- 5 GameDataService unit tests
- TypeScript clean across API and web

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add auth to /counts/check endpoint and document JWT secret

- Protect POST /counts/check with authenticateToken middleware to
  prevent unauthenticated access to session information
- Add inline documentation to .env noting the JWT secret should be
  replaced with a cryptographically random value before deployment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add rate limiting to all API routes to prevent brute-force attacks

- Add express-rate-limit dependency
- Strict 20 req/15min limit on auth endpoints
- General 500 req/15min limit on all other API endpoints
